pages.raw_text:
The fight between the local
Laundry Workers union and the
Laundrymen's association was
forced to a walkout of the union
members employed at the Eastern
laundry this noon. The manage
ment had previously declared for
an "open shop" and today 9 non
union employees of the Washington
laundry, which burned yesterday
were put to work in the Eastern.
The Washington work was also
brought in.


Under the rules Of their organiza-
ton the union workers could not
continue with nonunion employees
nor do nonunion work. At noon
the uion voted on a proposition to
quit unless the nonunion employees
were dismissed.


The entire union membership,
numbering IL women and girls and
5 men, voted unanimously to quit
If the request was refused. Assur-
ance from the management that
the association would furnish 5
workers at once to take their
places did not influence them in the
least. When their terms were re-
jected they went home.


Although e. T. Taylor and Geo.
Clason, who are in charge of the
Eastern, had set Saturday as the
day for deciding the question of an
"open shop" they stated today that
the union contract they signed a
few weeks ago would be abrogated
at once and the walkout followed.
Arrangements had already been
made to fill the places through the
association from employees thrown
out OF work by the fire yesterday.


Since its organization a few
months ago the Laundry Workers
union had established a 9-hour day
and secured considerably higher
wage scale than that paid the en-
ployes of the association concerns.
They prospered until the Eastern
laundry changed hands, and today's
developments confirm the theory
that the trust has secured control
of the place.


Although the union workers are
blacklisted and can hope for no
employment in the other local
laundries they are confident Of be-
ing back at work in a short time in
a union cooperative laundry which
organized labor is now busy pro-
noting. A committee from the
Central Labor union and another
from tho CtrrctnroI nu;ldipe,
Trades alliance are busy on the
proposition with every prospect of
speedily assembling the necessary
